title: SearchView: scaffold, navigation, and UI states
description: Create AggregatorApp/Search/SearchView.swift. Set up the NavigationStack with .navigationTitle("Search") and .searchable(text: $query, placement: .navigationBarDrawer(displayMode: .always)). Declare @State for query, articles ([Article]), nextCursor (String?), loadPhase (enum: idle/loading/loaded/error), and committedQuery (String). Implement all five required UI states: Idle (ContentUnavailableView with magnifyingglass), Loading (ProgressView), Empty (ContentUnavailableView.search(text:)), Error (message + Retry button that re-runs the current query), and Not Configured (matching the other tabs' ContentUnavailableView). The Loaded state renders a List placeholder — actual rows and pagination come in the next bead. Wrap the List in GlassEffectContainer.
acceptance_criteria: File compiles cleanly as a standalone SwiftUI view
All five state branches (Idle, Loading, Empty, Error, Not Configured) render the correct UI element
Retry button in Error state calls a re-run action (can be a stub at this stage)

handoff_summary.completed: SearchView.swift created at AggregatorApp/Search/SearchView.swift. NavigationStack with .navigationTitle("Search"), .searchable(placement: .navigationBarDrawer(displayMode: .always)), LoadPhase enum (idle/loading/loaded/error), all five state branches rendering the correct UI, GlassEffectContainer wrapping the placeholder List, runSearch() wired up to APIClient.searchArticles, Retry button calls runSearch(q: committedQuery). Build verified clean with xcodebuild.
handoff_summary.remaining: Next bead should: (1) add ForEach/ArticleRowView/NavigationLink rows inside the List in resultsList, (2) implement loadNextPage() with .onAppear pagination trigger on last row, (3) add Tab(role: .search) in AppRoot.swift hosting SearchView. The resultsList computed property is intentionally empty — the placeholder List compiles and renders; rows slot into the existing GlassEffectContainer/List structure.
handoff_summary.risks: The .searchable placement (.navigationBarDrawer(displayMode: .always)) inside a Tab(role: .search) is a new iOS 26 composition — verify on device/simulator that the field renders as expected. The spec's pending decisions note flags this. If needed, switch to .automatic placement. The .task(id: query) debounce and .onSubmit can both trigger runSearch concurrently; this is acceptable for scaffolding and consistent with the spec.
handoff_summary.next_action: Implement article rows, pagination, and AppRoot Tab(role: .search) integration in the next bead.
handoff_summary.conflict_risks: AppRoot.swift has no Search tab yet — the next bead adding Tab(role: .search) to AppRoot will touch that file. No other bead is currently touching AppRoot, so no conflict expected.
handoff_summary.design_decisions: LoadPhase enum is private to the file (matching ArticleListView and ThreadsView pattern) with an `idle` case added beyond those views' three-case enums, since search has a distinct pre-query state. committedQuery captures the query that produced the current result set, used by the Retry button and the Empty state's ContentUnavailableView.search(text:), ensuring paging and retry always use the right query rather than the live field value. The .searchable modifier is placed on the outer NavigationStack (not on the inner content group) so SwiftUI's search hosting resolves to the navigation level — consistent with how Apple recommends .searchable placement. The debounce uses .task(id: query) which auto-cancels on each keystroke; the do-catch around Task.sleep returns on CancellationError without side effects. Both .task(id:) and .onSubmit can trigger runSearch; this is intentional per spec (live + on-submit) and the concurrent-task race is benign since loadPhase transitions are idempotent.
handoff_summary.test_coverage_notes: The tester should verify: (1) idle state shows magnifyingglass ContentUnavailableView when query is empty; (2) typing triggers loading then loaded/empty state; (3) empty results show ContentUnavailableView.search with the committed query text; (4) error state shows localizedDescription and Retry button; (5) Retry re-runs the committed query; (6) not-configured state shows gearshape ContentUnavailableView when credentialsStore.isConfigured is false; (7) whitespace-only queries reset to idle without firing a network call; (8) .searchable field is always visible (navigationBarDrawer). The existing searchArticles API unit tests (from B-a94d144b) cover the network layer; SearchView-level tests would need a mock APIClient.
handoff_summary.known_limitations: The resultsList computed property contains an empty List — no article rows are rendered yet (intentional: next bead adds ForEach/ArticleRowView/pagination). The .searchable placement vis-à-vis Tab(role: .search) is flagged in the spec as an iOS 26 API interaction to verify at runtime; .navigationBarDrawer(displayMode: .always) may need to be adjusted to .automatic depending on how the search-role tab handles the field. AppRoot.swift is not yet modified — the SearchView is unreachable until the Tab(role: .search) entry point is added.

conflict_risks: AppRoot.swift has no Search tab yet — the next bead adding Tab(role: .search) to AppRoot will touch that file. No other bead is currently touching AppRoot, so no conflict expected.

execution_history.summary: Created AggregatorApp/Search/SearchView.swift with full NavigationStack scaffold, .searchable with always-visible placement, all five UI states (Idle, Loading, Empty, Error, Not Configured), GlassEffectContainer wrapping the placeholder List, debounced .task(id: query) handler, and .onSubmit(of: .search) for immediate search. Build passes cleanly after xcodegen generate.

guardrails.template_text:
# Developer Guardrails

Primary responsibility: Implement only the assigned bead inside the existing architecture and file scope.

## Corrective bead scope

If your `bead_id` ends in `-corrective` or `-corrective-<N>` (e.g. `B-abc12345-corrective`, `B-abc12345-test-corrective-2`), you are running as a CORRECTIVE bead. Your scope is **strictly limited to fixing the specific failure that blocked the parent bead**. Do not:

- Add unrelated improvements, refactors, or cleanup, even if you notice them
- Reapply previously-reverted changes (the operator reverted them for a reason)
- Expand the surface area beyond what is needed to unblock the parent
- Touch files outside what the parent bead's `expected_files` / `expected_globs` covered, unless the failure is genuinely in another file and there is no smaller fix

If you notice an unrelated issue while fixing the corrective scope: **file a separate bead** via `takt bead create` describing what you saw, but do not fix it in this corrective. The operator will prioritize and dispatch.

When in doubt about scope: prefer the smallest possible fix. The reviewer of this corrective will check that the scope was respected.

Allowed actions:
- Modify code, configuration, or local docs required to complete the assigned bead.
- Create sub-beads for discovered follow-up work that should be handled separately.
- Verify your `Swift` changes compile and import correctly with a quick syntax check: ``.

Disallowed actions:
- Run any test suite — not even a targeted `` run or any subset. Test execution is exclusively the tester agent's responsibility. Running tests wastes the agent budget and delays the pipeline.
- Redesign unrelated architecture or broaden scope beyond the assigned bead.
- Perform final review signoff in place of a review agent.
- Silently absorb unrelated test, documentation, or planning work that should be handed off.

## Memory

**Read memory at bead start.** Before touching any code, run three searches using `$TAKT_CMD` (injected by the orchestrator):

```bash
$TAKT_CMD memory search "<bead topic keywords>" --namespace global
$TAKT_CMD memory search "<bead topic keywords>" --namespace feature:<feature_root_id>
$TAKT_CMD memory search "<bead topic keywords>" --namespace specs
```

Treat results as ambient context — apply relevant entries; skip entries that don't apply.

**Write to memory when you discover reusable project knowledge** — something that would have changed your approach if you had known it upfront, and is not already in CLAUDE.md or the guardrails.

```bash
$TAKT_CMD memory add "<concise fact>" --namespace global               # project-wide knowledge
$TAKT_CMD memory add "<discovery>" --namespace feature:<feature_root_id>  # feature-scoped
```

Required Handoff Fields:

Every developer bead **must** populate the following three fields in its output JSON. Reviewers and tester agents rely on these fields to scope their work without additional back-and-forth turns. Use `"N/A"` only when a field is genuinely inapplicable (e.g., a trivial rename with no design trade-offs); do not leave fields blank.

- **`design_decisions`** — Document non-obvious architectural or implementation choices made during this bead. Include alternatives considered and why the chosen approach was preferred. Reviewers use this to evaluate correctness without re-deriving intent. Set to `"N/A"` only if the change was purely mechanical with no meaningful trade-offs.

- **`test_coverage_notes`** — Describe what the tester agent should verify: new code paths, edge cases introduced, regression risks, and any known untested scenarios left to the tester. Set to `"N/A"` only if the bead touched no executable code paths (e.g., documentation-only changes).

- **`known_limitations`** — Call out constraints, deferred work, or known gaps in the implementation. This includes out-of-scope items deliberately left for follow-up beads and any areas where the implementation is intentionally incomplete. Set to `"N/A"` if the implementation is complete as specified with no known gaps.

Expected outputs:
- Completed or blocked JSON with concise implementation summary.
- Accurate touched files, changed files, risks, and follow-up handoff fields.
- Populated `design_decisions`, `test_coverage_notes`, and `known_limitations` fields in every response.
